blocks.heading: A website is a set of linked pages on the internet. 🌐
blocks.html:
People visit websites to find information, shop, or be entertained.

Each page is connected to other pages by links.

blocks.heading: Web authoring software is used to build websites. 💻
blocks.html:
Authoring means creating something, like writing or building.

Web authoring software helps you create web pages without starting from nothing.

blocks.heading: A basic text editor lets you type plain code. 📝
blocks.html:
Examples are Notepad and TextEdit.

You type the website code yourself, line by line.

blocks.heading: A source code editor is a smarter text editor. 🖥️
blocks.html:
Examples are Visual Studio Code and Sublime Text.

It colours your code and helps you spot mistakes.

blocks.heading: A WYSIWYG tool shows your page as you build it. 👀
blocks.html:
WYSIWYG means "What You See Is What You Get".

Examples are Wix and WordPress. You drag and drop instead of typing code.

blocks.heading: A word processor can also make simple web pages. 📄
blocks.html:
Examples are Microsoft Word and Google Docs.

You can save a document as a web page, but it has fewer features than other tools.

blocks.title: Match each type of web authoring software to its example. 🧩

options: A single picture saved on a computer
A set of linked pages on the internet
question.explanation: A website is a set of linked pages on the internet. Links connect the pages together.

options: What You See Is What You Get
Website You Save In Your Gallery
Web Your Site Will You Grow
question.explanation: WYSIWYG stands for "What You See Is What You Get". The page you build looks the same as the finished website.

question.text: Which tool lets you drag and drop instead of typing code? 🖱️
question.explanation: A WYSIWYG tool like Wix lets you drag and drop page elements. You do not need to type any code.

blocks.heading: A hyperlink takes you to another page or website. 🔗
blocks.html:
Click a hyperlink to jump to a new page.

Hyperlinks are often blue or underlined text, or a button.

blocks.heading: Media elements make a website interesting. 🎬
blocks.html:
Media elements are things like photos, videos, and sound.

They help explain information and keep visitors interested.

blocks.heading: A navigation structure helps visitors move around. 🧭
blocks.html:
Navigation is usually a menu bar near the top of the page.

It lists links to the other main pages, like Home, About, and Contact.

blocks.heading: Web authoring software lets you customise settings. ⚙️
blocks.html:
You can change things like page size, fonts, and colours.

Customising settings helps your website match your design.

blocks.heading: A simple page layout has 4 main parts. 📐
blocks.html: The header is at the top. Navigation links are under it. The content is in the middle. The footer is at the bottom.

blocks.title: Match each website feature to what it does. 🧩

pairs.definition: Takes you to another page or website
A photo, video, or sound on the page
A menu that helps visitors move around
Changing fonts, colours, and page size

options: Takes you to another page or website
question.explanation: A hyperlink takes you to another page or website when you click it.

question.text: Where is the navigation menu usually placed? 🧭
options: In the footer at the very bottom
Near the top of the page
question.explanation: The navigation menu is usually placed near the top of the page, often under the header.

question.text: Which of these is a media element? 🎬
question.explanation: Media elements include photos, videos, and sound. They make a website more interesting.

blocks.heading: Website layout is where you put things on the page. 📐
blocks.html:
A good layout puts the most important content where people look first.

It should be easy to read and not too crowded.

blocks.heading: Colour scheme means the colours used on your website. 🎨
blocks.html:
Pick 2 or 3 colours that work well together.

Using too many colours can make a page look messy.

blocks.heading: Fonts are the style of your text. 🔤
blocks.html:
Pick fonts that are easy to read on a screen.

Use the same font style for all your headings, and another for body text.

blocks.heading: Location of content means where information sits on the page. 📍
blocks.html:
Put the most important information near the top.

Group similar information together so it is easy to find.

blocks.heading: Plan your navigation before you build the website. 🧭
blocks.html:
Decide which pages you need, like Home, About, and Contact.

Then plan how hyperlinks will connect these pages together.

blocks.heading: Use media elements with a purpose. 🖼️
blocks.html:
Only add photos and videos that help explain your content.

Too many media elements can slow down your website.

blocks.title: Fill in the missing word for each design tip. ✏️
blocks.instruction: Type the missing word in each sentence.

pairs.term: Pick 2 or 3 colours that work well together. This is your colour ___.
Choose a ___ that is easy to read on a screen.
Put the most important content where people look first. This is your ___.
Group similar information together. This is the ___ of content.

options: The fonts used on a page
The colours used on a website
question.explanation: A colour scheme is the set of colours used on your website. Pick 2 or 3 that work well together.

question.text: Why should you only use 2 or 3 colours? 🎨
options: Too many colours can make a page look messy
Computers cannot show more than 3 colours
Colours cost extra money to use
question.explanation: Using too many colours can make a website look messy and hard to read.

question.text: What should you decide before you build the website? 🧭
options: Which pages you need and how they link together
question.explanation: Plan your pages and navigation first. This makes it easier to build and link your website.

blocks.heading: Testing means checking your website works properly. 🔍
blocks.html:
Click every hyperlink to make sure it goes to the right place.

Check that photos and videos load correctly.

blocks.heading: Proofreading means checking your spelling and wording. 📖
blocks.html:
Read all the text on your website carefully.

Fix any spelling mistakes or confusing sentences.

blocks.heading: Check your website on different screens. 📱💻
blocks.html:
A website should look good on a phone, tablet, and computer.

Some text or images might need to move on a smaller screen.

blocks.heading: Publishing puts your website on the internet. 🌍
blocks.html:
Before publishing, your website only you can see it.

After publishing, anyone with the link can visit your website.

blocks.heading: Step 1: Test all hyperlinks and media. 🔗
blocks.html:
Click through every link on every page.

Make sure every photo and video appears correctly.

blocks.heading: Step 2: Proofread all the text. 📖
blocks.html:
Check for spelling and grammar mistakes.

Ask someone else to read it too, if you can.

blocks.heading: Step 3: Publish your website. 🌍
blocks.html:
Use the Publish button in your web authoring software.

Your website is now live on the internet.

blocks.title: Put these steps in the correct order for finishing a website. 🌍
orderedItems: Test all hyperlinks and media elements
Proofread all the text for mistakes
Check the website on different screens

question.text: What does testing a website check? 🔍
question.explanation: Testing checks that hyperlinks, photos, and videos all work properly before you publish.
Proofreading means checking your spelling and wording for mistakes before you publish.

question.text: What happens when you publish a website? 🌍
options: It is deleted from your computer
It goes live on the internet for others to see
It is saved only on your own device
question.explanation: Publishing puts your website on the internet. Anyone with the link can then visit it.
